Shotgun Sequencing
Determining the DNA Sequence from randomly generated clones.
For instance, one can restrict human genomic DNA, ligate it into a vector, and then just start sequencing every clone you can get your hands on.  This is the opposite of mapping before sequencing.

Comparative Genomics

Important Concepts
Relative gene numbers in model organisms
Gene similarities between species, within a species
Overall understanding of genome function
Comparative Genomics

Genome			Genes
pX174			       	11
Human mitochondria	37
Lambda virus		60
M. polymorpha chloroplast 	120
Haemophilus influenzae	1743
Mycoplasma genitalium  	470
E. coli				4288
S. cerevisiae			5885

Homologous sequences. Orthologs and Paralogs are two types of homologous sequences. Orthology describes genes in different species that derive from a common ancestor. Orthologous genes may or may not have the same function. Paralogy describes homologous genes within a single species that diverged by gene duplication
Homology is similarity due to inheritance from a common ancestor

Important terms/Concepts
Genome
Mapping Before Sequencing/Shotgun sequencing
C-paradox
Note the jump in Genome sizes from viruses, 	mitochondria, chloroplasts (thousands of bp) to 	bacterial genomes (millions of bp).
fX174-First genome sequenced, small phage, 11 	proteins
H. influenzae: First free-living organism, ~1,700 genes, 	~40% unknown function

Orthologous genes
Genes, defined by their sequence similarities, in two different species that arose from the same gene in the two species’ common ancestor
